title: Decadent Canadian Cauliflower Dumplings
description: Charred, molten cauliflower dumplings crowned with a rich cheese sauce, a low‑carb festive Canadian favorite
instructions: Pulse the cauliflower florets in a food processor until fine, then transfer the grated cauliflower to a bowl.
Add the beaten egg, cream cheese, cheddar cheese, onion salt, black pepper, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper to the cauliflower mixture and stir until well combined.
Shape the mixture into 12 small dumpling balls using your hands.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat until shimmering.
Place the dumplings in the skillet in a single layer and sear for 2 minutes on each side until a deep brown crust forms.
Transfer the skillet to a preheated 200°C oven and bake for 8 minutes to melt cheese inside.
Meanwhile, melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat, add minced garlic, and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour in the heavy cream, stir, and simmer for 3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Serve the charred dumplings topped with the molten cheese sauce, garnished with a sprinkle of smoked paprika.
